# Commit Message Rules

Required format:

`[category][stage]Commit description`

## Allowed categories

- feat -> New functionality
- fix -> Bug fix
- bug -> Critical bug fix

## Writing rules

- Use the imperative mood (for example: "Add", "Fix", "Refactor").
- Do not use past tense.
- Do not end the message with a period or ellipsis.
- Keep the full message at 50 characters or less.
- Keep one clear purpose per commit.
- Write specific and unambiguous descriptions.

## Restrictions

- Single-line message only.
- No commit body.
- No extra symbols, emojis, or additional text.

## Best-practice checklist

- Prefer small and focused commits.
- Ensure the commit message matches the actual diff.
- Avoid vague subjects like "update" or "fix stuff".
- Keep category and stage consistent with project workflow.

## Valid examples

`[feat][2]Add form input validation`
`[fix][1]Fix authentication cookie bug`
`[bug][3]Resolve API timeout crash`
